findings.reachability: Unauthenticated — any visitor whose request matches a proxy mapping or predefined path
findings.justification: get_client_ip() iterates HTTP_CLIENT_IP then HTTP_X_FORWARDED_FOR first, so an attacker can supply an arbitrary IP value that is then placed verbatim into X-Forwarded-For sent to the HubSpot CDN, bypassing any IP-based rate-limiting or allow-listing HubSpot applies to the forwarded IP.

detection_pattern.section_name: IP Spoofing: Untrusted X-Forwarded-For trusted over REMOTE_ADDR
detection_pattern.description: Code that reads HTTP_CLIENT_IP or HTTP_X_FORWARDED_FOR before REMOTE_ADDR for client IP determination allows any attacker to inject a forged IP into forwarded headers or access-control logic.

findings.reachability: Any code (plugin or theme) that can register a WordPress filter hook — i.e., any installed plugin with write capability, or admin users via code execution
findings.justification: Both the mapping fetch URL (get_plugin_mappings_api_url) and the proxy destination base URL (get_proxy_base_url) are built through apply_filters() calls. A malicious or compromised plugin hooking leadin_sites_proxy_cdn or leadin_plugin_mappings_api_url can redirect all outbound wp_remote_get() calls to an arbitrary internal or external host, achieving SSRF or data exfiltration.

detection_pattern.section_name: SSRF: wp_remote_get target controlled by filter hook
detection_pattern.description: When wp_remote_get (or wp_remote_post) is called with a URL derived from apply_filters(), any hooked plugin can redirect the outbound request to an arbitrary destination including internal services.

findings.reachability: Unauthenticated — any visitor triggering a predefined path pattern match (/_hcms/, /hs/, /hubfs/, /hs-fs/, /cs/c/, /e3t/)
findings.justification: When request_uri matches a PREDEFINED_PATH_PATTERNS regex (line 246-249), the raw request URI is returned directly as proxy_path with no further validation, and is concatenated onto get_proxy_base_url() at line 164; if the base URL base allows path traversal sequences (e.g. /hs/../../internal-service), the constructed target_url could point to unintended paths on the destination host. esc_url_raw() prevents scheme-level injection but does not collapse path traversal.

detection_pattern.section_name: SSRF: Proxy path traversal in concatenated target URL
detection_pattern.description: When a request URI is appended directly to a fixed base URL without normalizing path traversal sequences (../../), attackers may reach unintended paths on the destination server.

findings.reachability: Unauthenticated — any visitor whose request reaches the proxy path and matches a mapping
findings.justification: Line 227 emits header('X-HS-WP-Plugin-Proxy-URL: ' . $target_url) without stripping \r\n; while esc_url_raw() on REQUEST_URI removes most CRLF, the base URL is assembled from filter results and option values that are not CRLF-sanitized, leaving a theoretical header injection window distinct from the request-URI path that is already sanitized.

detection_pattern.section_name: Response Header Injection: unsanitized value in header()
detection_pattern.description: Calls to header() that concatenate attacker-influenced or filter-derived strings without stripping CR/LF characters can allow response header injection or HTTP response splitting.

findings.reachability: Unauthenticated — any visitor that triggers a proxy request where the remote response contains script content
findings.justification: The raw body of the remote wp_remote_get() response is echoed directly (echo $body; with a phpcs:ignore suppressing the escaping warning). Although this is intended behaviour for a reverse proxy, there is no Content-Type gating or sanitization: if an attacker can influence what HubSpot returns (e.g. via a stored XSS on HubSpot pages) or if the proxy CDN URL is redirected via a filter hook, arbitrary HTML/script executes under the WordPress site's origin.

detection_pattern.section_name: XSS: Unescaped remote response body echoed to browser
detection_pattern.description: Directly echoing the body of a wp_remote_get() response without sanitization passes through any XSS payload the remote server returns, executing under the WordPress site's origin.

findings.reachability: Unauthenticated or authenticated — any visitor with non-WordPress cookies set by other plugins whose request triggers the proxy
findings.justification: filter_wordpress_cookies() only strips cookies with three specific prefixes (wordpress_, wp-settings-, wp_woocommerce_). Cookies set by other plugins (e.g. wc_session_cookie, PHPSESSID, WooCommerce cart cookies, security plugin cookies) are forwarded to the external HubSpot CDN, potentially leaking session state and user data to a third party.

detection_pattern.section_name: Cookie Leakage: Incomplete cookie stripping before proxy forward
detection_pattern.description: Proxy implementations that forward Cookie headers with only a partial denylist may leak sensitive session cookies from other plugins to the external proxy destination.
